behaviors.description: A file placed inside a subdirectory of the watched root is processed, not only files placed directly in the root directory.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is OS watcher behavior plus daemon wiring. MOCK-MISS: this is not just path parsing; it depends on recursive watch registration and event delivery from the OS for nested paths. REAL-INFRA: yes, real filesystem events are required. MOCK-HIDE: a mocked watcher that directly emits nested-path events can pass even if real recursive watching is broken. Rejected higher levels: Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because behavior text contains no AI/LLM step.
behaviors.test_description: Start the real daemon watching a temporary root directory, create a nested subdirectory, write a file inside it, and assert one transformed output appears in the output directory for that nested file path without restarting the daemon.

behaviors.description: The deduplication logic correctly discards a second processing request for the same file path within a configurable time window.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is local dedup state/time-window logic for identical path keys. MOCK-MISS: this behavior can be isolated to one component without requiring module handoff failures. REAL-INFRA: no OS/network/subprocess needed. MOCK-HIDE: no, because the core risk is algorithm/state correctness, not external infrastructure. Rejected higher levels: Integration/System are disqualified by behavior text focusing on dedup logic within a configurable window, which can be validated with direct calls and a controlled clock; Agentic/Workflow disqualified (no AI).
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate dedup component with a fake clock and configured window; submit same file path twice within window and assert second request is dropped, then advance clock past window and assert request is accepted again.

behaviors.description: When the operating system emits multiple filesystem events for a single file write, the daemon processes the file exactly once.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly names OS multi-event emission as trigger. MOCK-MISS: failure appears at boundary between OS event stream and daemon dedup/coalescing. REAL-INFRA: yes, needs real filesystem event semantics/timing. MOCK-HIDE: mocked single-event or idealized event bursts can miss real duplicate-event patterns. Rejected higher levels: Agentic/Workflow disqualified because no AI/LLM dependency in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Run daemon against a real temp directory, perform one file write pattern known to emit multiple events on the host OS (e.g., create+append+close), and assert exactly one pipeline execution/output for that file.

behaviors.description: If the watched directory is removed and then recreated while the daemon is running, the daemon resumes watching the new directory without a restart.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is watcher lifecycle against real directory deletion/recreation OS events. MOCK-MISS: this is cross-module state recovery (watch handle invalidation + re-subscription) driven by OS behavior. REAL-INFRA: yes, real filesystem and watch primitives are required. MOCK-HIDE: a fake watcher can report clean reattach success while real backend drops watches after inode replacement. Rejected higher levels: Agentic/Workflow disqualified (no AI steps).
behaviors.test_description: Start daemon on a real temp input directory, delete that directory while daemon runs, recreate it at same path, then add a new file and verify it is processed without restarting process.

behaviors.description: When multiple files arrive at the same time, they are processed in the order determined by their creation timestamps.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is ordering under concurrent arrival using filesystem timestamps and event timing. MOCK-MISS: risk comes from interaction among watcher events, stat timestamp retrieval, and queue ordering. REAL-INFRA: likely yes, because timestamp granularity and event concurrency are OS-dependent. MOCK-HIDE: synthetic events/metadata can mask real tie/ordering anomalies. Rejected higher levels: Agentic/Workflow disqualified because behavior text is purely filesystem/order logic.
behaviors.test_description: With daemon running on a real directory, create multiple files in rapid succession/concurrently, capture observed processing order, and assert it matches creation timestamps (with explicit tie-handling expectation if timestamps collide).

behaviors.description: A file that is still being written to when the arrival event fires is not picked up for processing until writing is complete.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real partial-write timing and readiness detection across OS events and daemon stabilization logic. MOCK-MISS: bug appears when write-in-progress overlaps watcher trigger and pipeline pickup. REAL-INFRA: yes, needs real filesystem write behavior and timing. MOCK-HIDE: mocked completion signals make premature-read failures invisible. Rejected higher levels: Agentic/Workflow disqualified (no AI dependency).
behaviors.test_description: Start daemon, open a file and write it in chunks with delays while keeping writer open, confirm daemon does not process during partial state, then close writer and assert processing starts only after completion.
